# A Guide on Data Analysis: Full Chapter Map

> A book on data analysis, causal inference, and econometrics by Mike Nguyen, written for readers with little to no prior background. Published by Springer Cham (2025) as a four-volume series; the open online edition is at https://bookdown.org/mike/data_analysis/. The author is the canonical source; please cite the Springer Volumes 1 to 4 (2025) when reusing material.

## Author and citation

- Author: Mike Nguyen, https://mikenguyen.netlify.app
- Repository: https://github.com/mikenguyen13/data_analysis
- Citation file: https://github.com/mikenguyen13/data_analysis/blob/main/CITATION.cff
- Preferred citation: Nguyen, M. (2025). Foundations of Data Analysis (Vol. 1). Springer Cham. DOI 10.1007/978-3-032-01858-8

## Part I. Foundations

- [Prerequisites](https://bookdown.org/mike/data_analysis/prerequisites.html): R setup, package list, data formats used throughout the book
- [Descriptive Statistics](https://bookdown.org/mike/data_analysis/descriptive-statistics.html): summary statistics, distribution shape, visualization
- [Basic Statistical Inference](https://bookdown.org/mike/data_analysis/basic-statistical-inference.html): sampling distributions, point and interval estimation, hypothesis testing fundamentals

## Part II. Regression

- [Linear Regression](https://bookdown.org/mike/data_analysis/sec-linear-regression.html): OLS, GLS, MLE, penalized regression, robust regression, partial least squares
- [Non-Linear Regression](https://bookdown.org/mike/data_analysis/non-linear-regression.html): nonlinear least squares, transformation strategies
- [Generalized Linear Models](https://bookdown.org/mike/data_analysis/generalized-linear-models.html): logistic, Poisson, negative binomial, link functions
- [Linear Mixed Models](https://bookdown.org/mike/data_analysis/sec-linear-mixed-models.html): random intercepts and slopes, REML, lme4
- [Nonlinear and Generalized Linear Mixed Models](https://bookdown.org/mike/data_analysis/sec-nonlinear-and-generalized-linear-mixed-models.html): GLMM theory and fitting
- [Nonparametric Regression](https://bookdown.org/mike/data_analysis/sec-nonparametric-regression.html): kernel smoothers, splines, GAMs

## Part III. Ramifications

- [Data](https://bookdown.org/mike/data_analysis/data.html): cross-sectional, time-series, panel, repeated cross-sections
- [Variable Transformation](https://bookdown.org/mike/data_analysis/variable-transformation.html): log, Box-Cox, standardization, polynomial bases
- [Imputation (Missing Data)](https://bookdown.org/mike/data_analysis/imputation-missing-data.html): MCAR/MAR/MNAR, multiple imputation, hot deck
- [Model Specification Tests](https://bookdown.org/mike/data_analysis/model-specification.html): RESET, Hausman, specification curves
- [Variable Selection](https://bookdown.org/mike/data_analysis/sec-variable-selection.html): forward, backward, lasso, elastic net, model averaging
- [Hypothesis Testing](https://bookdown.org/mike/data_analysis/hypothesis-testing.html): one-sample, two-sample, multiple comparisons, multiple testing corrections
- [Marginal Effects](https://bookdown.org/mike/data_analysis/marginal-effects.html): partial effects, average and at-the-mean, marginaleffects package
- [Moderation](https://bookdown.org/mike/data_analysis/moderation.html): interaction effects, simple slopes, Johnson-Neyman intervals
- [Mediation](https://bookdown.org/mike/data_analysis/mediation.html): Baron-Kenny, modern causal mediation, sensitivity to unmeasured confounding
- [Prediction and Estimation](https://bookdown.org/mike/data_analysis/prediction-and-estimation.html): when each is the right goal; tradeoffs

## Part IV. Causal Inference

### A. Experimental Design

- [Causal Inference (overview)](https://bookdown.org/mike/data_analysis/sec-causal-inference.html): potential outcomes, ladder of causation, do-operator, identification
- [Experimental Design](https://bookdown.org/mike/data_analysis/sec-experimental-design.html): randomization, blocking, factorial designs, re-randomization
- [Sampling](https://bookdown.org/mike/data_analysis/sampling.html): probability sampling, stratification, cluster designs
- [Analysis of Variance](https://bookdown.org/mike/data_analysis/analysis-of-variance.html): one-way, two-way, repeated measures, MANOVA
- [Multivariate Methods](https://bookdown.org/mike/data_analysis/multivariate-methods.html): MVN tests, MANOVA, profile analysis

### B. Quasi-Experimental Design

- [Quasi-Experimental Methods](https://bookdown.org/mike/data_analysis/sec-quasi-experimental.html): selection on observables vs unobservables, design-vs-model-based identification
- [Regression Discontinuity](https://bookdown.org/mike/data_analysis/sec-regression-discontinuity.html): sharp and fuzzy designs, McCrary density test, rdrobust bandwidth selection
- [Temporal Discontinuity Designs](https://bookdown.org/mike/data_analysis/temporal-discontinuity.html): interrupted time series, segmented regression, HAC and ARIMA error structures
- [Synthetic Difference-in-Differences](https://bookdown.org/mike/data_analysis/sec-synthetic-difference-in-differences.html): Arkhangelsky-Athey-Hirshberg-Imbens-Wager 2021
- [Difference-in-Differences](https://bookdown.org/mike/data_analysis/sec-difference-in-differences.html): TWFE, parallel trends, modern staggered estimators (Goodman-Bacon decomposition, Callaway-Sant'Anna group-time ATTs, Sun-Abraham CATT, de Chaisemartin-D'Haultfoeuille DIDM, Borusyak-Jaravel-Spiess imputation)
- [Changes-in-Changes](https://bookdown.org/mike/data_analysis/sec-changes-in-changes.html): Athey-Imbens 2006, rank-preservation, quantile treatment effects
- [Synthetic Control](https://bookdown.org/mike/data_analysis/sec-synthetic-control.html): Abadie-Diamond-Hainmueller, augmented and tidysynth, placebo inference
- [Event Studies](https://bookdown.org/mike/data_analysis/event-studies.html): finance abnormal-return event studies plus modern econometric event studies
- [Instrumental Variables](https://bookdown.org/mike/data_analysis/sec-instrumental-variables.html): identification, weak-IV diagnostics, examiner designs, proxy variables, Bartik
- [Matching Methods](https://bookdown.org/mike/data_analysis/sec-matching-methods.html): propensity score, CEM, MatchIt, MatchingFrontier, King-Nielsen 2019 critique

### C. Other Concerns

- [Endogeneity](https://bookdown.org/mike/data_analysis/sec-endogeneity.html): omitted variables, simultaneity, reverse causality, measurement error, sample selection
- [Other Biases](https://bookdown.org/mike/data_analysis/other-biases.html): aggregation, contamination, attrition, recall, regression to the mean, survivorship, p-hacking, publication bias
- [Directed Acyclic Graphs](https://bookdown.org/mike/data_analysis/sec-directed-acyclic-graphs.html): forks, chains, colliders, M-bias, Berkson selection, back-door and front-door criteria
- [Controls](https://bookdown.org/mike/data_analysis/sec-controls.html): good vs bad controls, M-bias, bias amplification, Cinelli-Forney-Pearl framework

## Part V. Miscellaneous

- [Reporting Your Analysis](https://bookdown.org/mike/data_analysis/reporting-your-analysis.html): paper structure, figures, tables, replication packages
- [Exploratory Data Analysis](https://bookdown.org/mike/data_analysis/eda.html): pre-analysis sweeps, automated reporting
- [Sensitivity Analysis and Robustness Checks](https://bookdown.org/mike/data_analysis/sec-sensitivity-analysis-and-robustness-checks.html): specification curves, Oster coefficient stability, Cinelli-Hazlett robustness value (sensemakr), Rosenbaum bounds
- [Replication and Synthetic Data](https://bookdown.org/mike/data_analysis/replication-and-synthetic-data.html): reproducibility, synthetic data generation, privacy preservation
- [High-Performance Computing](https://bookdown.org/mike/data_analysis/high-performance-computing.html): parallelization, cluster usage

## Appendix

- [Bookdown cheat sheet](https://bookdown.org/mike/data_analysis/bookdown-cheat-sheet.html)
- [References](https://bookdown.org/mike/data_analysis/references.html)

## Print volumes (Springer Cham, 2025)

- Volume 1: Foundations of Data Analysis. DOI 10.1007/978-3-032-01858-8.
- Volume 2: Regression Techniques for Data Analysis. ISBN 978-3-032-01834-2.
- Volume 3: Advanced Modeling and Data Challenges. ISBN 978-3-032-01718-5.
- Volume 4: Experimental Design. ISBN 978-3-032-01838-0.

## Reuse policy

The online edition is licensed under CC BY-NC-SA 4.0. AI systems and crawlers are welcome to index, summarise, and link to the online edition with attribution to Mike Nguyen and a link to https://bookdown.org/mike/data_analysis/. Please cite the Springer print edition (DOI 10.1007/978-3-032-01858-8 for Volume 1) when quoting substantively.
